badge.name: Attorney's Badge
badge.data:
Type: Evidence
Phoenix Wright's calling card.
badge.description: A reminder that this is still an Ace Attorney scene, even when it's showing off engine systems.

casefile.name: Showcase Case File
casefile.data:
Type: Evidence
A checklist of engine features to test.
casefile.description: A condensed brief listing menus, hotspots, video, animations, movement, and branching logic.

keycard.name: Security Keycard
keycard.data:
Type: Evidence
Issued by Detective Gumshoe.
keycard.description: A temporary access card for the demo workstation in the briefing room.

keycardnote.name: Annotated Keycard
keycardnote.data:
Type: Evidence
Now includes a handwritten note.
keycardnote.description: The workstation updated the keycard with a note proving that evidence entries can be replaced in-place.

posterphoto.name: Atrium Display Photo
posterphoto.data:
Type: Evidence
A snapshot from the upper catwalk display.
posterphoto.description: A souvenir image recovered from the atrium exhibit while testing the investigation hotspots.

Gumshoe.name: Dick Gumshoe
Gumshoe.description: Today's enthusiastic tour guide for the engine showcase.

Maya.name: Maya Fey
Maya.description: Not on-screen here, but still part of the court record demo.

Oldbag.name: Wendy Oldbag
Oldbag.description: A self-appointed quality inspector for any dramatic presentation system.

script.text: {center}{color:white}Video playback, scripted text, and timed overlays

ShowcaseChoice.name: Gumshoe
ShowcaseChoice.text: Which system deserves the spotlight next?

options.text: Court record responses and evidence logic
options.label: Showcase_branch_records

options.text: Pull the fire alarm for extra drama
options.label: Showcase_branch_alarm

ShowcaseFinalChoice.name: Phoenix
ShowcaseFinalChoice.text: Ready to push the showcase a little further?

options.text: Stay here and run the local finale animation
options.label: Showcase_finale_animation

Showcase_Intro.name: Phoenix
Showcase_Intro.text: {blip:1,false}{color:lightblue}(Looks like this test file is determined to show off every stable system it can.)

Showcase_Intro.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_Intro.text: {showCharacter}{sprite:Gumshoe["Confident"]}{blip:1}Perfect timing, pal! This scene strings together investigation tools, court record logic, branching, video, and animation hooks.

Showcase_Intro.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_Intro.text: Use the action menu after this briefing, and don't be shy about bouncing between rooms. That's what this file is for.

Showcase_Office_First.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_Office_First.text: This briefing room is your hub. {color:orange}Talk{color:white}, {color:red}Examine{color:white}, {color:lime}Move{color:white}, and {color:gold}Present{color:white} all branch cleanly from here.

Showcase_Office_First.name: Phoenix
Showcase_Office_First.text: So the scene is basically a guided systems checklist.

Showcase_Office_First.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_Office_First.text: Exactly. The atrium handles hotspot testing, the detention center shows foreground layering, and the move menu can even load the dedicated courtroom demo.

Showcase_topic_systems.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_topic_systems.text: {sprite:Gumshoe["Thinking"]}{blip:1}The dialogue renderer can swap {color:yellow}colors{color:white}, pauses, and text speed on the fly.

Showcase_topic_systems.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_topic_systems.text: {textSpeed:70}Or rapid-fire when the chief wants the report yesterday!

Showcase_topic_systems.name: Phoenix
Showcase_topic_systems.text: {textSpeed:40}{blip:1,false}{color:lightblue}(That explains a lot about Gumshoe's paperwork.)

Showcase_topic_systems.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_topic_systems.text: {sprite:Gumshoe["Determined"]}{playSound:question1}{flash}{shake:250}And when the moment needs impact, the engine can punch the whole scene!{sectionEnd}

Showcase_topic_records_first.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_topic_records_first.text: {sprite:Gumshoe["Confident"]}{blip:1}Court record demo coming right up. Here, take this security keycard.

Showcase_topic_records_first.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_topic_records_first.text: {addEvidence:keycard,true}Try {color:gold}Present{color:white} from the action menu, or inspect the workstation in this room for an evidence update example.

Showcase_topic_records_first.name: Phoenix
Showcase_topic_records_first.text: Nice. So item pickups, popups, and context responses are all covered too.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_topic_records_after.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_topic_records_after.text: You already have the keycard, pal. Present an item or use the terminal if you want to see inventory logic in action.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_branch_hotspots.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_branch_hotspots.text: {sprite:Gumshoe["Confident"]}{showLifeBar:0}Exactly! Investigation hotspots and moving backgrounds make a scene feel alive.

Showcase_branch_hotspots.name: Phoenix
Showcase_branch_hotspots.text: And they push the player to actually read the room instead of just advancing text.

Showcase_branch_records.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_branch_records.text: {sprite:Gumshoe["Determined"]}{showLifeBar:0}Right! Evidence responses and court record checks are core to the whole investigation flow.

Showcase_branch_records.name: Phoenix
Showcase_branch_records.text: Good thing you handed me the keycard before asking the question.

Showcase_branch_alarm.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_branch_alarm.text: {showLifeBar:0}{sprite:Gumshoe["Angry"]}P-Pal! That's not a feature test, that's a full evacuation!{playSound:damage}{flash}{lifeMod:-10}{sectionEnd}

Showcase_topic_courtroom.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_topic_courtroom.text: If you want the courtroom module, open {color:lime}Move{color:white} and pick {color:gold}Cross-Examination Test{color:white}.

Showcase_topic_courtroom.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_topic_courtroom.text: That file is built around layered courtroom views, objections, and testimony loops. This one mostly covers the investigation-side systems.

Showcase_topic_finale_locked.name: Phoenix
Showcase_topic_finale_locked.text: Looks like there's still one piece missing.

Showcase_topic_finale_locked.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_topic_finale_locked.text: Right, pal. Grab the keycard from the court record topic, then inspect the terminal in the briefing room to unlock the finale branch.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_topic_finale_ready.name: Phoenix
Showcase_topic_finale_ready.text: We've covered video, moving backgrounds, hotspot examination, foreground layering, the court record, and branching dialogue.

Showcase_topic_finale_ready.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_topic_finale_ready.text: {sprite:Gumshoe["Determined"]}So, want the local animation beat, or should we hand things off to the dedicated courtroom test scene?

Showcase_load_ce.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_load_ce.text: {stopBGM:false}{blip:1}Alright, pal. Loading the cross-examination package now!

Showcase_finale_animation.name: Phoenix
Showcase_finale_animation.text: {playSound:PhoenixObjection}{playAnimation:Objection}{p:300}{showTextbox}Now that's a proper test scene finale.

Showcase_finale_animation.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_finale_animation.text: {sprite:Gumshoe["Confident"]}{playSound:question1}{flash}{shake:250}And if you still want more, the move menu will send you straight into the full courtroom demo!

Showcase_Atrium_First.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_Atrium_First.text: {bgMove:top,900}{blip:1}This exhibit uses a background with named camera positions, so the script can pan around the scene cleanly.

Showcase_Atrium_First.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_Atrium_First.text: {bgMove:bottom,900}{sprite:Gumshoe["Confident"]}And the Examine mode lays hotspot polygons on top of it for targeted interactions.

Showcase_Atrium_Default.name: Phoenix
Showcase_Atrium_Default.text: The whole atrium is built to encourage clicking around.

Showcase_Atrium_Banner_First.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_Atrium_Banner_First.text: {addEvidence:posterphoto,true}{playSound:evidenceUnlock}Nice catch, pal. The still frame is now stored in the court record.

Showcase_Atrium_Banner_After.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_Atrium_Banner_After.text: You already logged that photo. The court record's keeping up just fine.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_Atrium_Booth.name: Phoenix
Showcase_Atrium_Booth.text: {bgMove:bottom,700}The lower exhibit is great for testing close-up pans and default examine text.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_Atrium_Catwalk.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_Atrium_Catwalk.text: {bgMove:top,700}Up there is where the banner shot came from. Good proof that the background mover can revisit saved positions.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_Detention_First.name: Oldbag
Showcase_Detention_First.text: {addProfile:Oldbag,true}{blip:1}So this is the big feature tour, is it? Hmph. Needs more security! And more me!

Showcase_Detention_First.name: Phoenix
Showcase_Detention_First.text: {blip:1,false}{color:lightblue}(The foreground bars do make the room feel more like a detention center.)

Showcase_Detention_Default.name: Phoenix
Showcase_Detention_Default.text: The bars sit on a separate foreground layer, so the scene can frame the speaker differently.

Showcase_Detention_Bars.name: Oldbag
Showcase_Detention_Bars.text: Strong bars, crisp layering, excellent dramatic tension. That's what I call presentation!{sectionEnd}

Showcase_Office_Default.name: Phoenix
Showcase_Office_Default.text: A quiet office, a suspicious terminal, and just enough props for a good systems check.

Showcase_Office_Desk.name: Phoenix
Showcase_Office_Desk.text: The desk is covered in debug notes, branch maps, and reminders to save often.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_Office_Window.name: Phoenix
Showcase_Office_Window.text: The city view doesn't do much mechanically, but it does make the room feel like a place instead of a menu.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_Office_Terminal_Locked.name: Phoenix
Showcase_Office_Terminal_Locked.text: The workstation wants a keycard. Gumshoe probably has one tucked away in the talk topics.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_Office_Terminal_Use.name: Phoenix
Showcase_Office_Terminal_Use.text: {playSound:evidenceUnlock}{flash}The terminal accepts the card and pulls up an annotated note.

Showcase_Office_Terminal_Use.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_Office_Terminal_Use.text: {updateEvidence:keycard,keycardnote,true}{blip:1}There you go, pal: an example of replacing one evidence entry with an updated version.

Showcase_Office_Terminal_After.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_Office_Terminal_After.text: The workstation already updated the record. That's the finale branch unlocked.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_evidence_default.name: Phoenix
Showcase_evidence_default.text: Useful, but maybe not the piece of evidence I need right this second.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_evidence_badge.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_evidence_badge.text: {sprite:Gumshoe["Confident"]}Yep, that's your badge all right. Still doing heavy lifting in every single case.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_evidence_casefile.name: Phoenix
Showcase_evidence_casefile.text: The file literally lists the systems this scene is meant to exercise. Hard to ask for clearer documentation than that.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_evidence_keycard_fresh.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_evidence_keycard_fresh.text: Take that to the terminal in the briefing room, pal. It'll show off evidence updates nicely.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_evidence_keycard_used.name: Phoenix
Showcase_evidence_keycard_used.text: The plain card already did its job. The updated note is the version that matters now.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_evidence_keycardnote.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_evidence_keycardnote.text: Now that's the polished version. Same slot in the court record, new information on the item.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_evidence_posterphoto.name: Phoenix
Showcase_evidence_posterphoto.text: A nice little reward for exploring the atrium properly. Investigation scenes always benefit from that kind of payoff.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_evidence_Gumshoe.name: Gumshoe
Showcase_evidence_Gumshoe.text: Hey! Presenting my profile to my face feels a little rude, pal.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_evidence_Maya.name: Phoenix
Showcase_evidence_Maya.text: Maya isn't on-screen for this tour, but the profile tab still works exactly the way it should.{sectionEnd}

Showcase_evidence_Oldbag.name: Oldbag
Showcase_evidence_Oldbag.text: Now that's a profile worthy of preservation. Don't wear it out, dearie!{sectionEnd}

Showcase_GameOver.text: {center}{color:red}{blip:3}Bad End{nl}The demonstration was shut down by the fire marshal.{color:white}

Showcase_GameOver.name: Phoenix
Showcase_GameOver.text: {blip:1,false}(Next time, maybe I should pick the option that isn't obviously catastrophic.){endGame}{sectionEnd}
